mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer income and spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns. This is an always-on trend signal where higher PAYEMS levels are associated with higher future equity prices.
failure_condition: If over a rolling 3-year window the average forward 20-day return following months with positive PAYEMS growth is not positive, or if Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS level and 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises to or above 4.5%, it signals a weakening labor market and increased recession risk, prompting defensive positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in economic contraction.
failure_condition: If in months where UNRATE is above 4.5%, the subsequent 5-day forward return is not negative on average (t-test p>0.05) or if the hit rate of negative returns is less than 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate (>=4.83%) combined with still-rising core inflation (CPI MoM >=0.2%) signals that monetary policy is not yet restrictive enough to tame inflation, increasing the probability of further tightening and weighing on equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return in months meeting the trigger condition is not negative, or if the signal does not outperform a baseline buy-and-hold over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%), declining housing starts signal weaker housing demand and broader economic slowdown, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as real estate weakness spills into consumption and investment.
failure_condition: If during periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, a monthly decline in HOUST does not lead to lower forward 60-day returns compared to periods with an increase, or if the Rank IC of the signal remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production of at least 0.5% signals an abrupt slowdown in manufacturing activity and aggregate supply, consistent with impending recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors adjust growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return following months with INDPRO decline >=0.5% is positive or not statistically significantly negative (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline for three consecutive months while payrolls (PAYEMS) continue to grow, a divergence emerges: labor income still supports consumption, but the weakening spending trend foreshadows a slowdown that eventually hurts corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declining for 3 consecutive months AND PAYEMS still growing month-over-month
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return in months meeting the divergence condition is not negative on average, or if the signal does not have a positive Sharpe ratio during such periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPIAUCSL < 300), rising retail sales (RSAFS) reflect genuine consumer demand without overheating concerns, which boosts corporate earnings and equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If in months when CPIAUCSL < 300, the Spearman correlation between RSAFS monthly growth and forward 5-day return is not positive (rho < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that drives corporate revenues and earnings, creating a persistent tailwind for equity prices. This always-on trend implies that higher levels of consumer spending predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS monthly growth and forward 5-day return is not positive over any 5-year rolling window.
